engineGuide.h2: Range Rover Mainline Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Range Rover Mainline across all generations (L322, L405, L460), with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The most common engine in UK Mainline models, the 306DT powered over 60% of L322 and early L405 Vogues before widespread timing chain failures made it the #1 replacement enquiry driver.
compatibleVariants: Vogue, Vogue SE, HSE, Autobiography, Westminster, L322 Vogue, L322 Autobiography, L405 Vogue, L405 Vogue SE, L405 HSE, L405 Autobiography
commonFailures: Timing chain wear (60,000-80,000 miles) - chain stretch triggers dashboard warning and engine rattle
Crankshaft failure (70,000-100,000 miles) - sudden bottom end failure often requiring full replacement
Oil pump failure (80,000-120,000 miles) - oil starvation leads to bearing seizure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306DT Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The updated SDV6 variant introduced in 2015 brought higher power output and revised injector timing, but retained the same fundamental architecture and timing chain vulnerability.
compatibleVariants: Vogue SE, HSE Dynamic, Autobiography, L405 Vogue SE, L405 Autobiography, L405 HSE Dynamic
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (50,000-70,000 miles) - tensioner collapse causes chain slap and valve timing errors
Crankshaft bearing wear (80,000-110,000 miles) - low oil pressure triggers knock and eventual seizure
Oil pump belt failure (60,000-90,000 miles) - belt disintegration blocks oil pickup
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306D1 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The final evolution of the SDV6 fitted to late L405 models, with improved crankcase ventilation and revised timing chain guides to address earlier failures.
compatibleVariants: Autobiography, Vogue SE, L405 Autobiography, L405 Vogue SE
commonFailures: Timing chain guide fracture (70,000-90,000 miles) - plastic guide failure allows chain to jump teeth
EGR cooler leak (50,000-70,000 miles) - coolant loss and white smoke from exhaust
DPF regeneration failure (60,000-100,000 miles) - blocked filter triggers limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306D2 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Diesel
entries.history: Ford Lion-derived V8 diesel introduced with the L405, offering 700Nm torque and refined cruising, but complex emissions systems and turbocharger failures generate significant replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Westminster, Vogue SE, Autobiography, L322 Westminster, L405 Vogue SE, L405 Autobiography, SDV8 Autobiography
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure (80,000-110,000 miles) - oil starvation causes turbine shaft play and boost loss
EGR cooler leak (60,000-90,000 miles) - internal coolant loss and white smoke
Injector seal failure (70,000-100,000 miles) - diesel smell in engine bay and carbon buildup around injectors
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJD-V8 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Diesel
entries.history: Ford Lion V8 diesel that launched the L322 TDV8, offering refined V8 power in a diesel package, but crankshaft failures and turbo complexity make it a specialist replacement proposition.
compatibleVariants: TDV8, Vogue TDV8, L322 Vogue TDV8, L322 Autobiography TDV8
commonFailures: Crankshaft failure (90,000-120,000 miles) - sudden bottom end failure, often without warning
Turbocharger failure (80,000-110,000 miles) - bearing wear and boost pressure loss
Oil cooler leak (70,000-100,000 miles) - coolant and oil mixing causes emulsion
entries.cta: Get quotes for 368DT Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: JLR's four-cylinder Ingenium diesel fitted to entry-level L405 models, offering improved fuel economy but suffering from timing chain and DPF issues common to the Ingenium family.
compatibleVariants: HSE, Vogue, L405 HSE, L405 Vogue
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch (50,000-70,000 miles) - chain noise and camshaft correlation faults
DPF blockage (60,000-80,000 miles) - regeneration failure triggers limp mode
EGR valve carbonisation (40,000-60,000 miles) - sticky valve causes rough idle and emissions faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTA Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol Supercharged
entries.history: Jaguar-Land Rover's definitive 5.0L supercharged V8, producing up to 567hp in SV guise. Sought-after by performance owners despite cooling system weaknesses and supercharger wear.
compatibleVariants: SV Autobiography, SVAutobiography Dynamic, SVAutobiography LWB, L405 SVAutobiography, L405 SVAutobiography Dynamic
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler failure (60,000-80,000 miles) - rattling noise on idle and boost loss under load
Cooling system leak (50,000-70,000 miles) - coolant crossover pipe failure causes sudden coolant loss
Timing chain guide wear (70,000-100,000 miles) - chain rattle and cam timing faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ133 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol Supercharged
entries.history: Higher-output version of the AJ133, factory-rated at 508PS (500hp) for pre-SV Autobiography models, sharing the same supercharged V8 architecture and cooling system vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: Autobiography, Vogue SE, L405 Autobiography, L405 Vogue SE
commonFailures: Water pump failure (50,000-70,000 miles) - coolant leak and overheating risk
Thermostat housing crack (60,000-80,000 miles) - external coolant leak
Supercharger bearing wear (70,000-90,000 miles) - whining noise and boost pressure fluctuation
entries.cta: Get quotes for 508PS Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: BMW M62B44 V8 carried over from pre-facelift L322 models, renowned for hydraulic valve lifters and VANOS complexity, but sought after by heritage Range Rover specialists.
commonFailures: VANOS seal failure (70,000-90,000 miles) - rattling noise and loss of low-end torque
Valve stem seal wear (80,000-110,000 miles) - blue smoke on startup and oil consumption
Timing chain guide fracture (90,000-120,000 miles) - chain rattle and potential valve interference
entries.cta: Get quotes for 448S2 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated Jaguar-derived V8 fitted to early L322 facelift models, replacing the BMW M62 with JLR's own 4.4L unit before supercharged variants took over.
commonFailures: Cooling system leak (60,000-80,000 miles) - water pump and thermostat housing failures
Coil pack failure (50,000-70,000 miles) - misfire and rough running
Oil filter housing gasket leak (60,000-80,000 miles) - external oil leak
entries.cta: Get quotes for 448PN Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 4.2L Petrol Supercharged
entries.history: Jaguar's supercharged 4.2L V8 introduced in facelift L322 models, offering 390hp and genuine performance cred, though early supercharger couplers and timing chain guides are documented weaknesses.
compatibleVariants: Vogue SE, Autobiography, L322 Vogue SE, L322 Autobiography
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler wear (60,000-80,000 miles) - rattle on idle, boost loss under acceleration
Timing chain guide fracture (70,000-90,000 miles) - chain rattle and cam timing faults
Coolant leak from front crossover pipe (50,000-70,000 miles) - sudden coolant loss and overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for 428PS Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol Supercharged
entries.history: Supercharged 3.0L V6 petrol fitted to entry-level petrol L405 models, offering 340hp but rarely seen in the UK market - replacement demand is low but specialist supply is available.
compatibleVariants: Vogue, HSE, L405 Vogue, L405 HSE
commonFailures: Supercharger coupler failure (60,000-80,000 miles) - rattling noise on idle
Intercooler leak (50,000-70,000 miles) - boost pressure loss and reduced power
Coolant leak (60,000-80,000 miles) - thermostat housing and water pump failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306PS Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L PHEV
entries.history: Ingenium 2.0L turbo petrol combined with 85kW electric motor and 13.1kWh battery, delivering 398hp in P400e form. Complex hybrid integration means fewer independent rebuilders, but electric drive unit failures are increasingly reported.
compatibleVariants: P400e, L405 P400e, Autobiography P400e, HSE P400e
commonFailures: EGR valve failure (40,000-60,000 miles) - rough idle and emissions warning lights
Coolant pump failure (50,000-70,000 miles) - battery thermal management faults
High-voltage battery degradation (60,000-80,000 miles) - reduced electric range and charging faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ20P4 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L MHEV Petrol
entries.history: BMW-sourced B58-based 3.0L straight-six with 48V mild hybrid system, fitted to L460 models from 2022. Relatively new to the UK market, so failure data is still emerging.
compatibleVariants: SE, Vogue, L460 SE, L460 Vogue
commonFailures: Coolant pump failure (30,000-50,000 miles) - hybrid cooling system leak
48V battery management faults (reported from 40,000 miles) - mild hybrid system warnings
Turbocharger actuator failure (emerging reports) - boost control faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for PT306 Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L MHEV Petrol
entries.history: BMW N63 twin-turbo V8 with 48V mild hybrid assistance, producing 523hp in L460 Autobiography models. BMW's hot-vee design introduces heat management challenges carried over from earlier N63 generations.
compatibleVariants: Autobiography, SV, L460 Autobiography, L460 SV
commonFailures: Turbocharger actuator failure (40,000-60,000 miles) - boost pressure faults and reduced power
Coolant leak from hot-vee crossover (30,000-50,000 miles) - internal valley leak causes coolant loss
Timing chain wear (emerging reports at 50,000+ miles) - chain noise and cam correlation codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for N63B44T Range Rover Mainline engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Range Rover Mainline Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Range Rover Mainline - particularly L322 and L405 generations with 3.0L TDV6/SDV6 (306DT, 306D1) diesel engines - has a documented reputation for timing chain and crankshaft failures that drive the majority of UK replacement enquiries. 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ133) owners face cooling system and supercharger wear issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EnginesMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below we break down the four most common Mainline engine failures, what they cost to fix at dealer versus specialist, and when replacement is the only sensible option.

problems.group: Range Rover Mainline Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Mainline Timing Chain Failure - Why TDV6/SDV6 Diesel Engines Fail

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit (chains, tensioners, guides, sprockets), oil pump inspection, sump removal to access rear chains
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Only viable if caught early with rattle but no cam correlation faults

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After chain jump or valve strike
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt 306DT/306D1 unit, upgraded timing components, new oil pump, water pump, full gasket set, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles. Required if valve timing lost or piston damaged. Includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Range Rover Mainline’s 3.0L diesel is rattling at cold start but hasn't thrown a camshaft correlation code, a preventative chain replacement at a specialist saves £800-£1,300 versus dealer pricing and buys another 60,000+ miles. On a 2012-2017 L405 worth £8,000-£15,000, a full rebuilt engine at specialist rates (£4,500-£7,500) is financially viable. If the chain has already jumped and the engine is seized or non-starting, replacement is your only option - a repair of bent valves costs nearly as much as a rebuilt unit.
problems.cta: Compare 306DT and 306D1 rebuilt engine prices for your Range Rover Mainline

problems.h4: Range Rover Mainline Crankshaft Failure - The Catastrophic TDV6/SDV6 Weak Point

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Crankshaft Regrind & Bearing Replacement - Caught at first knock
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, crank regrind, oversized bearings, oil pump replacement, full gasket set, bolt set
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Only viable if caught before seizure and crank is salvageable

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After seizure or crank damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt 306DT/306D1 unit, upgraded oil pump, improved bearing materials, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles. Required if crank is scored beyond regrind spec. Includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: Crankshaft failure on a Range Rover Mainline 3.0L diesel is typically discovered when the engine knocks and seizes within 20-50 miles. By that point, the crank is often scrap. Unless you catch the first faint knock and stop driving immediately, a regrind is rarely possible. For the vast majority of owners, a rebuilt engine from a specialist (£4,500-£7,800 supply & fit) is the most cost-effective route - dealer pricing pushes £10,000+, which often exceeds the vehicle's value on older L322 models.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 306DT crankshaft failure replacement on your Range Rover Mainline

problems.h4: Range Rover Mainline Supercharger Failure - 5.0L AJ133 V8 Coupler & Cooling Issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, replace coupler and needle bearings, fresh oil, refit with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Perfect for rattle-only symptoms with no internal damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat housing, coolant crossover pipes, expansion tank, fresh coolant
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Preventive or response to first leak - avoids head gasket failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt supercharger unit, upgraded coupler, bearings, seals, tested
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles. Required if internal bearing failure or housing damage

problems.recommendation: On a 5.0L supercharged Range Rover Mainline, the rattle you hear at idle is almost always the coupler - not a terminal failure. A specialist coupler rebuild for £650-£1,100 is the smart move. For cooling, replace the crossover pipes and water pump proactively at 60,000 miles during a service; a £900-£1,600 specialist investment prevents a £7,000+ overheated engine replacement. Dealers charge nearly double for the same work.
problems.cta: Compare AJ133 supercharger rebuild and cooling system prices for your Range Rover Mainline

problems.group: Range Rover Mainline DPF & EGR Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Mainline DPF & EGR Failure - Post-2015 Diesel Emissions System Problems

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic forced regen, chemical DPF clean, pressure sensor reset, road test
repairOptions.longevity: 20,000-30,000 miles. Viable for first-time blockage with no structural damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, clean EGR valve, new gaskets, coolant refill, adaption reset
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Required if internal leak detected - ignores at risk of hydrolock

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace DPF filter, reset differential pressure sensor, full regen cycle, road test
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles. Required if DPF cracked or ash loading exceeds cleanable limit

problems.recommendation: If your Range Rover Mainline diesel is flashing a DPF warning but still drives, a specialist forced regen (£150-£300) usually clears it - dealer pricing doubles that. For repeated blockages, the underlying issue is short journeys; a DPF replacement won't fix the root cause. EGR cooler leaks are common on 306D1 engines from 50,000 miles; the white smoke and coolant loss won't improve without replacement. A specialist EGR cooler swap for £450-£750 is half the dealer price.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DPF and EGR repairs on your Range Rover Mainline diesel

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Range Rover Mainline
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain rattle, sudden bottom end knock, supercharger whine, or DPF warnings don't have to mean the end of your Range Rover Mainline. EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mainline engine specialists who supply rebuilt units with upgraded components designed to address the original failure points. Every engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery available. Stop guessing at dealer prices - compare specialist quotes and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Range Rover Mainline Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Range Rover Mainline and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Range Rover Mainline Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Range Rover Mainline across L322, L405 and L460 generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years, power outputs and common failure notes.

groups.title: Range Rover Mainline Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L diesel has been the backbone of the Range Rover Mainline range since the L322 facelift in 2006. Initially launched as the TDV6 (245hp), it evolved into the SDV6 with outputs up to 306hp across L405 models. The later Ingenium straight-six (D300/D350) introduced mild-hybrid technology for improved efficiency and lower emissions from 2020 onward .
commonFailurePoints: crankshaft bearing failure on early 306DT units
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Mainline 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 3.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.6L TDV8 was introduced as a premium diesel option for the L322 facelift, offering V8 refinement and 272hp from Ford's Lion V8 diesel. While sought-after for its torque delivery and smoothness, the complexity of the twin-turbo V8 arrangement makes replacement more expensive than the six-cylinder alternatives .
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Mainline 3.6L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 4.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 4.4L SDV8 replaced the 3.6L TDV8 in late L322 production and carried through the L405 generation. Producing 334-339hp and 700Nm of torque, it offered effortless long-distance cruising. The 4.4L diesel remained available until the L460 generation switched to the six-cylinder Ingenium range .
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Mainline 4.4L diesel engines

groups.title: Range Rover Mainline Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.4L V8 served as the entry-level petrol engine for early L322 models. Two distinct units exist: the BMW M62 (2002-2005) with 286hp and the naturally-aspirated Jaguar-derived unit (2005-2009) with 300hp. Both are now considered heritage engines with specialist rebuilders rather than mass-market supply .
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Mainline 4.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 4.2L Supercharged Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2L supercharged V8 was the first performance petrol option in the L322, producing 390-400hp. Fitted exclusively to facelift L322 Vogue SE and Autobiography models, it offered genuine sporting credentials in a full-size luxury SUV but suffers from supercharger coupler and timing chain guide wear .
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Mainline 4.2L supercharged petrol engine prices

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 5.0L Supercharged Petrol Engines
items.description: The definitive Range Rover performance engine, the 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ133) powers SV Autobiography and high-spec L405 models with 510-567hp. Renowned for effortless power but notorious for cooling system weaknesses and supercharger coupler failure. A small number of naturally aspirated 5.0L units (508PS, 375hp) exist in pre-SV Autobiography models .
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Mainline 5.0L supercharged petrol engine prices

groups.title: Range Rover Mainline Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 2.0L PHEV Engines
items.description: The P400e plug-in hybrid combined a 2.0L Ingenium turbo petrol engine with an 85kW electric motor and 13.1kWh battery, delivering 398hp and electric-only range of around 30 miles. Fitted to late L405 models (2018-2021), it offered lower Benefit-in-Kind tax for fleet operators but complex hybrid systems mean fewer independent rebuilders .
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Mainline 2.0L PHEV engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 3.0L MHEV Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced with the L460 generation from 2022, the 3.0L straight-six mild-hybrid petrol (PT306) uses BMW-sourced B58 architecture with 48V MHEV assistance. Producing 395hp, it represents the modern petrol offering for the latest Range Rover Mainline models .
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Mainline 3.0L MHEV petrol engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Mainline 4.4L MHEV Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship L460 petrol engine is a 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (N63B44T) with 48V mild-hybrid assistance, producing 523hp in Autobiography and SV models. The BMW-sourced "hot-vee" design places turbochargers inside the V-angle, introducing heat management challenges documented across earlier N63 generations .
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Mainline 4.4L MHEV petrol engine availability

families: AJ20P4 - 2.0L Ingenium turbo petrol + 85kW electric motor (P400e)
13.1kWh battery powers electric motor for zero-emission urban driving
Petrol engine automatically engages for higher speeds or when battery depleted
Regenerative braking recovers energy to recharge the battery during driving
foundIn: L405 P400e, Autobiography P400e, HSE P400e (2018-2021)
importantNotes: Lower Benefit-in-Kind tax made P400e popular with fleet and company car drivers
Complex hybrid systems mean fewer independent rebuilders than conventional engines
Battery health inspection essential during engine replacement - degradation affects performance
Electric motor and inverter replacement requires specialist hybrid-trained technicians

items.title: Range Rover Mainline Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines

N63B44T - 4.4L V8 MHEV petrol (Autobiography/SV, 523hp, BMW N63 architecture)
48V belt-integrated starter-generator recovers braking energy and assists acceleration
No plug-in capability - battery charges automatically during driving
Electric boost fills torque gaps between gear shifts and reduces turbo lag

MHEV component failure (48V battery, starter-generator) can trigger engine warning lights
Engine replacement requires hybrid system disconnection and recalibration
Specialist MHEV-trained technicians recommended - not all garages have the equipment

engineYears.h2: Range Rover Mainline Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Track every major engine change across the Range Rover Mainline from its 1970 origins to the latest L460 models - organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

keyChanges: 1970: Launch of three-door Range Rover with detuned Rover V8 (135hp)
1981: Four-door body introduced - the first major refinement step
1988: VM Motori 2.4L turbodiesel (Vogue Turbo D) arrives - 12 world records broken
1992: Long Wheelbase Vogue LSE launched with 4.2L Rover V8
knownFor: Buick-derived Rover V8 - lightweight aluminium, used for 26 years
Early models suffered overheating in hot climates - twin thermo fans added 1981
VM diesels rare today - specialist parts availability limited
Classic Range Rover now a collector's item - values rising for rust-free examples
years.cta: Get quotes for Classic Range Rover 1970-1996 replacement engines

keyChanges: 1994: P38A launched replacing Classic Range Rover
GEMS engine management on petrol V8s
1999: Thor engine management replaces GEMS on petrol models
knownFor: Air suspension issues - compressor and bag failures common
BMW M51 diesel robust but underpowered for heavy Range Rover
4.6L V8 suffers slipped cylinder liners at higher mileage
Electronics (BECM) notoriously complex and failure-prone
years.cta: Get quotes for P38A Range Rover 1994-2002 replacement engines

keyChanges: 2002: L322 launched with BMW-sourced engines and electronics
BMW M62 V8 replaces Rover V8 family
2005 facelift - Jaguar engines begin replacing BMW units
knownFor: BMW engineering = more reliable than early Rover V8s
M62 VANOS seal failure causes rattle and power loss
M57 Td6 is underpowered (174hp) for heavy L322 - adequate not exciting
2005 facelift ended BMW engine era in Range Rover
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2005 L322 Range Rover replacement engines

keyChanges: 2005: Jaguar AJ-V8 engines replace BMW M62
2006: 3.6L TDV8 diesel (Ford Lion) launched - major performance upgrade
knownFor: 3.6L TDV8 transformed diesel performance - 272hp and 640Nm torque
4.2L supercharged V8 is a performance bargain - watch for supercharger coupler wear
Early 3.6L TDV8 crankshaft failures reported (90k-120k miles)
Jaguar V8s generally reliable but cooling system needs attention
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2009 L322 Range Rover replacement engines

years.preview: L322 Late Models: SDV6 & 5.0L Supercharged Arrive
keyChanges: 2009: Major L322 facelift - new interior and revised exterior
knownFor: 3.0L TDV6/SDV6 timing chain failures begin appearing - the #1 replacement driver
AJ133 5.0L supercharged - cooling crossover pipe failure risk
SDV8 (4.4L) considered most reliable L322 diesel - but rare
Peak L322 before L405 switch - values holding strong
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 L322 Range Rover replacement engines

years.preview: L405 Launch: Aluminium Body, Same Engines
keyChanges: 2012: L405 launched at Paris Motor Show - 420kg weight saving
Same 3.0L TDV6/SDV6 and 5.0L supercharged V8 carry over
Revised Terrain Response 2 with auto mode
knownFor: New lightweight platform - improved fuel economy and handling
Same 3.0L diesel engine failures as L322 - timing chain tensioner weakness
Early L405s share L322 failure patterns - no improvement yet
SDV6 (292hp) popular but chain failures common 60k-85k miles

P400e PHEV offers 30-mile electric range - lower BIK tax for company users

48V mild hybrid system on all engines

faq.h2: Range Rover Mainline Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Range Rover Mainline engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt 3.0L TDV6/SDV6 costs £4,500-£7,800 supply & fitted. The 4.4L SDV8 runs £5,000-£8,500, while the 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ133) costs £6,000-£11,000. Used engines start lower but carry higher risk.
keyPoints: Full rebuilt engine supply & fit: £4,500-£11,000 depending on engine code
Used engine supply only: £1,500-£4,500 - but warranty limited or none
Reconditioned units average £3,200-£7,500 with 12-month warranty
Fitting alone adds £600-£1,200 to any engine price
SDV6 timing chain replacement is £900-£1,600 - much cheaper than full failure
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Range Rover Mainline engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Which Range Rover Mainline engines are most reliable?
items.answer: The 4.4L BMW M62 V8 (2002-2005) and naturally aspirated 5.0L AJ V8 are the most dependable. The newer 3.0L Ingenium straight-six MHEV shows strong early reliability, while the 4.4L SDV8 is the safest diesel bet.
keyPoints: BMW 4.4L M62 V8 - widely regarded as one of the strongest Range Rover engines ever fitted
Naturally aspirated 5.0L AJ V8 - more reliable than supercharged version, runs cooler
4.4L SDV8 diesel - lower failure rate than TDV6, excellent torque delivery
3.0L Ingenium inline-six MHEV - excellent early reliability, avoid four-cylinder Ingenium
items.cta: Get quotes for the most reliable Range Rover Mainline engine options

items.question: What causes the TDV6 crankshaft to fail in Range Rover Mainline diesels?
items.answer: The 3.0L TDV6 (306DT) has a Ford-derived bottom-end design flaw causing a 2-3% crankshaft failure rate regardless of service history. Failure is sudden and often catastrophic.
keyPoints: TDV6 crankshaft failure hits 2-3% of units - independent of maintenance
Catastrophic failure often occurs between 70,000-110,000 miles with no warning
SDV6 has lower 1% failure rate than standard TDV6
Once crank seizes, full replacement is only option - regrind rarely possible
Early warning sign is bottom-end knock - stop driving immediately
items.cta: Compare 306DT crankshaft failure replacement prices for your Mainline

items.question: How long do Range Rover Mainline 3.0L diesel engines actually last?
items.answer: Research data shows 68% of SDV6 units reach 150,000 miles with proactive timing chain and EGR intervention. Only 41% of TDV6 units hit 150k due to crankshaft and timing chain design flaws.
keyPoints: SDV6 (3.0L diesel): 68% reach 150,000 miles with proper maintenance
TDV6 (3.0L diesel): Only 41% reach 150,000 miles - avoid high-mileage examples
Timing chain service at 100k (TDV6) or 112k (SDV6) is non-negotiable
EGR cooler fails on 68% of units between 90,000-130,000 miles
Crankshaft failure independent of maintenance - purely design flaw
items.cta: Find a rebuilt SDV6 engine for your Range Rover Mainline

items.question: What are the symptoms of timing chain failure on a Range Rover Mainline?
items.answer: A metallic rattle on cold start is the earliest sign. Left unchecked, the engine throws camshaft correlation codes (P0017/P0018), then misfires, loses power, and eventually jumps timing - destroying valves and pistons.
keyPoints: Brief metallic rattle or chatter on cold start - first warning sign
Check engine light with P0017-P0019 codes - cam/crank correlation mismatch
Rough idle, hesitation, loss of power as timing drifts out of specification
Deferring chain service risks piston-to-valve collision - catastrophic damage
Preventative replacement at 100-112k miles costs £900-£1,600 vs £5k+ for new engine
items.cta: Compare 306DT timing chain replacement quotes for your Mainline

items.question: Is the 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ133) reliable?
items.answer: The AJ133 is a charismatic, powerful engine but has two well-documented weak points: coolant crossover pipes (plastic, fail every 4-5 years) and timing chain tensioners. Frequent oil changes (5,000-7,500 miles) prevent timing issues.
keyPoints: Plastic coolant crossover pipes fail every 50,000-70,000 miles - sudden coolant loss risk
Replace cooling system proactively at 60k miles - specialist cost £900-£1,600
Frequent oil changes (5,000-7,500 miles) prevent timing chain tensioner failure
Aftermarket metal coolant lines available as permanent fix
Supercharger coupler rattle at idle is common - snout rebuild costs £650-£1,100
items.cta: Get quotes for AJ133 5.0L supercharged V8 replacement engines

items.question: Should I rebuild my Range Rover Mainline engine or replace it?
items.answer: Rebuild only for minor failures (top end, gaskets, EGR) below £2,500. For timing chain jump, crankshaft failure, or seized engine, replacement is cheaper and comes with warranty. Full rebuild on a TDV6 costs nearly as much as a warranted replacement.
keyPoints: Light rebuild (gaskets, EGR, ancillaries): £2,500-£3,500 - often worth it
Full rebuild after major failure: £4,000-£6,000 - approaches replacement cost
Replacement with warranted rebuilt unit: £4,500-£7,800 - new engine, same price
Replaced engines include 12-month warranty - rebuilds often don't
For TDV6 with bottom end damage: replacement is only sensible option
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Mainline engine rebuild vs replacement costs

items.question: What is the real-world fuel economy of Range Rover Mainline engines?
items.answer: Diesel SDV6 achieves 34-38 mpg officially, 35 mpg real-world. Petrol V8s return 23.5-29 mpg. The modern 3.0L MHEV petrol manages around 25 mpg. PHEV offers 40-50 miles electric range then 31 mpg.
keyPoints: 3.0L SDV6 diesel: 34-38 mpg official, 35 mpg real-world, 40+ mpg on motorway runs
5.0L supercharged V8: 23.5-29 mpg official - expect low 20s in real use
4.4L SDV8 diesel: around 32-35 mpg depending on driving
P400e PHEV: 40-50 miles electric range, then approximately 31 mpg
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Mainline diesel or petrol replacement engines

items.question: How do I find my Range Rover Mainline engine code?
items.answer: Check your V5C logbook under 'Vehicle details' for the engine number. The code is the first 4-6 characters (e.g., 306DT). Alternatively, open the driver's door and check the chassis plate or use our registration lookup above for instant identification.
keyPoints: V5C registration document (logbook) - 'Engine number' field shows the code
Driver's door chassis plate - engine code on VIN sticker
Service history documents - engine type recorded at each service
Under-bonnet compliance sticker - near front crossmember on L405
Use registration lookup above - instant engine code identification
items.cta: Use our free registration tool to identify your Range Rover Mainline engine code

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Range Rover Mainline?
items.answer: If the body and chassis are solid and the vehicle is otherwise well-maintained, a warranted replacement engine at £4,500-£7,800 is often better value than a used replacement car with unknown history. On a 2006-2009 L322, replacement may exceed vehicle value.
keyPoints: L405 (2012-2021) worth £12k-£25k - replacement engines are good value
L322 (2002-2012) worth £4k-£10k - replacement only viable for clean examples
Replacement engine comes with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
A used replacement car has unknown service history - same engine risks
If body, gearbox, suspension are sound - replacement makes financial sense
items.cta: Check Range Rover Mainline engine replacement value for your specific model year
